description_en: The six pseudo-fawasil in سورة هود are serialized into one compact sentence, with the first and second occurrence labels embedded inside the running prose rather than set out as separate items.
impact_en: The extraction is reliable, but it requires editorial separation of the six phrases and of the occurrence notes attached to the two “سوف تعلمون” items.

description_en: The first transmitted head, “خَبِير,” appears without a displayed numeral.
impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer treats this as head 1 while preserving the witness’s lack of a displayed numeral.

description_en: The witness jumps from displayed 53 “لَا تنْظرُون” to displayed 55 “مُسْتَقِيم”.
impact_en: The missing displayed 54 corresponds to the Kufi-only disputed head “تشركون”, which is not part of the later-Madani sequence.

description_en: After displayed 81 “سجيل”, the local witness omits four heads and resumes with an unnumbered “بحفيظ” before displayed 86 “الرشيد”.
impact_en: The recovered later-Madani sequence restores the omitted interval explicitly as “بِبَعِيد / مُحِيط / مفسدين / مُؤمنين”, with the gap kept visible through null source-line fields.

description_en: Near the end of the block, the witness runs “١١٧ - أَجْمَعِينَ / ١١٩ - للْمُؤْمِنين / ١٢٠ - منتظرون / ١٢٢ - تَعْمَلُونَ” and then ends with a blank terminal line “١٢ - ٣”.
impact_en: The missing displayed 118 and 121 correspond to the disputed heads “مختلفين” and “إِنَّا عاملون”, neither of which belongs to the later-Madani sequence; the final blank line is preserved as display metadata only.

description_en: Because the transmitted block begins unnumbered, skips displayed 54, loses a four-head interval before unnumbered “بحفيظ”, later skips displayed 118 and 121, and ends with a blank terminal line, the displayed numerals do not function as a clean independent ordinal layer.
impact_en: The dataset stores recovered later-Madani ordinals together with witness display numbers where present, and records the restored heads explicitly.
